chunks:
# ==============================================================================
# IVD Recipe: AI Classifier Agent Pattern
# ==============================================================================
# Version: 1.0
# Created: 2026-01-23
# Category: agent_patterns
# ==============================================================================

recipe:
  name: "AI Classifier Agent Pattern"
  version: "1.0"
  category: "agent_patterns"
  created: "2026-01-23"
  author: "Leo Celis"
  
  description: |
    Standard pattern for building AI agents that classify inputs into categories
    using LLM-based understanding with quality validation. This pattern is 
    reusable across different classification domains (leads, support tickets, 
    content, facts, etc.) and provides a structured approach to maintaining 
    classification quality over time.
    
    Key characteristics:
    - Uses LLM for context understanding (not keyword matching)
    - Includes validation layer for quality assurance
    - Monitors performance metrics continuously
    - Handles edge cases and failure modes
  
  applies_to:
    - "Categorizing items that require context understanding"
    - "Classification where quality thresholds are critical"
    - "Multi-class or multi-label classification problems"
    - "Domain-specific classification (leads, tickets, content, facts)"
  
  when_to_use:
    - "Classification requires understanding context, not just keywords"
    - "Quality/accuracy requirements are high (typically >= 80%)"
    - "Input data is unstructured or semi-structured text"
    - "Categories may evolve based on business needs"
    - "Explainability of classification decisions is important"
  
  when_NOT_to_use:
    - "Simple rule-based classification is sufficient (< 10 rules)"
    - "Real-time latency requirement < 100ms (LLM calls add latency)"
    - "No quality/accuracy requirements (any answer is acceptable)"
    - "Classification is purely numeric/quantitative"
    - "Training data exists and traditional ML would work better"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# TEMPLATE SECTIONS
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

intent_template:
  summary: "[Domain] classifier agent for [use case]"
  goal: "Classify [items] into [categories] for [stakeholder] to [purpose]"
  success_metric: "[Metric name (accuracy/precision/recall)] >= [target]%"
  stakeholders:
    - "[Primary stakeholder role]"
    - "[Secondary stakeholder role]"

constraints_template:
  - name: "classification_accuracy"
    requirement: "accuracy >= [0.80-0.95 typical]"
    typical_values: "0.80 minimum, 0.90 good, 0.95+ excellent"
    test: "tests/test_[module]_accuracy.py::test_classification_accuracy"
    consequence_if_violated: "[Stakeholder] receives incorrect classifications, leading to [business impact]"
    
  - name: "response_time"
    requirement: "p95 latency < [1-3s typical for LLM-based]"
    typical_values: "1s excellent, 2s good, 3s acceptable"
    test: "tests/test_[module]_performance.py::test_latency"
    consequence_if_violated: "User experience degraded, [downstream process] delayed"

mplate:
  - name: "classification_accuracy"
    requirement: "accuracy >= [0.80-0.95 typical]"
    typical_values: "0.80 minimum, 0.90 good, 0.95+ excellent"
    test: "tests/test_[module]_accuracy.py::test_classification_accuracy"
    consequence_if_violated: "[Stakeholder] receives incorrect classifications, leading to [business impact]"
    
  - name: "response_time"
    requirement: "p95 latency < [1-3s typical for LLM-based]"
    typical_values: "1s excellent, 2s good, 3s acceptable"
    test: "tests/test_[module]_performance.py::test_latency"
    consequence_if_violated: "User experience degraded, [downstream process] delayed"
    
  - name: "cost_per_classification"
    requirement: "average cost < $[0.01-0.10 typical]"
    typical_values: "Depends on LLM model and prompt complexity"
    test: "tests/test_[module]_cost.py::test_cost_tracking"
    consequence_if_violated: "Operational costs exceed budget"

rationale_template:
  decision: "Use LLM-based classification with [specific approach: single prompt / chain of thought / ensemble]"
  reasoning: |
    - LLM provides context understanding beyond keyword matching
    - Validation layer ensures quality before output
    - [Domain-specific reasoning about why this approach fits]
    - Accepted trade-off: Higher cost/latency for better accuracy
  evidence: "playground/[domain]_classification_analysis_[date].ipynb"
  stakeholder: "[Role] ([Name])"
  date: "[YYYY-MM-DD]"

alternatives_template:
  - name: "rule_based_classification"
    typical_rejection_reason: "Too brittle, requires constant maintenance as categories evolve"
    when_it_works: "< 10 clear rules, categories stable, no context understanding needed"
    
  - name: "traditional_ml_classifier"
    typical_rejection_reason: "Insufficient labeled training data or overfitting on small dataset"
    when_it_works: "Large labeled dataset available (>10K examples), categories well-defined"
    
  - name: "keyword_matching"
    typical_rejection_reason: "Fails on edge cases, doesn't understand context or nuance"
    when_it_works: "Very simple categories with obvious keywords, low accuracy acceptable"
    
  - name: "manual_human_classification"
    typical_rejection_reason: "Doesn't scale, slow, expensive, inconsistent across humans"
    when_it_works: "Very low volume (< 10/day), high cost acceptable, ultimate accuracy needed"

risks_template:
  - condition: "If accuracy drops below [threshold - 0.05]"
    impact: "high"
    action: "Alert team, review recent classifications, investigate model/prompt drift"
    monitor: "CloudWatch/Datadog dashboard - [dashboard_name]"
    severity: "high"
    
  - condition: "If latency exceeds [threshold * 2]"
    impact: "medium"
    action: "Check LLM service health, consider fallback to cached results"
    monitor: "APM latency metrics"
    severity: "medium"
    
  - condition: "If cost per classification > [budget * 1.5]"
    impact: "medium"
    action: "Review prompt efficiency, consider cheaper model for simple cases"
    monitor: "Cost tracking dashboard"
    severity: "medium"
    
  - condition: "If categories evolve significantly"
    impact: "medium"
    action: "Retrain/update prompt

t"
    monitor: "CloudWatch/Datadog dashboard - [dashboard_name]"
    severity: "high"
    
  - condition: "If latency exceeds [threshold * 2]"
    impact: "medium"
    action: "Check LLM service health, consider fallback to cached results"
    monitor: "APM latency metrics"
    severity: "medium"
    
  - condition: "If cost per classification > [budget * 1.5]"
    impact: "medium"
    action: "Review prompt efficiency, consider cheaper model for simple cases"
    monitor: "Cost tracking dashboard"
    severity: "medium"
    
  - condition: "If categories evolve significantly"
    impact: "medium"
    action: "Retrain/update prompts, validate on new category examples"
    monitor: "Category distribution tracking"
    severity: "medium"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# IMPLEMENTATION PATTERN
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

implementation_pattern:
  directory_structure: |
    agent/[classifier_name]/
    ├── agent.py                      # Main classifier agent (inherits BaseAgent)
    ├── {agent}_intent.yaml           # Intent artifact (uses this recipe, e.g. classifier_intent.yaml)
    ├── classifier.py                 # Core classification logic
    ├── validator.py                  # Output validation logic
    ├── prompts.py                    # LLM prompts (or use prompt/[name]_prompts.py)
    ├── utils.py                      # Helper functions
    └── tests/
        ├── test_accuracy.py          # Accuracy validation tests
        ├── test_performance.py       # Latency and throughput tests
        ├── test_cost.py              # Cost tracking tests
        └── test_edge_cases.py        # Edge case handling tests
  
  code_structure: |
    # agent.py
    class ClassifierAgent(BaseAgent):
        def __init__(self, config):
            super().__init__(config)
            self.classifier = Classifier(config)
            self.validator = Validator(config)
            
        async def process(self, input_data):
            # 1. Validate input
            if not self._validate_input(input_data):
                return error_response
                
            # 2. Prepare context for LLM
            context = self._prepare_context(input_data)
            
            # 3. Call LLM for classification
            classification = await self.classifier.classify(context)
            
            # 4. Validate output
            if not self.validator.is_valid(classification):
                # Handle invalid classification
                classification = self._handle_invalid(classification)
            
            # 5. Track metrics
            self._track_metrics(classification)
            
            # 6. Return result
            return classification_response
  
  key_files:
    - file: "agent.py"
      purpose: "Main agent class, orchestrates classification workflow"
      key_functions:
        - name: "process"
          purpose: "Main entry point for classification requests"
        - name: "_prepare_context"
          purpose: "Prepare input for LLM (formatting, context building)"
        - name: "_track_metrics"
          purpo

                classification = self._handle_invalid(classification)
            
            # 5. Track metrics
            self._track_metrics(classification)
            
            # 6. Return result
            return classification_response
  
  key_files:
    - file: "agent.py"
      purpose: "Main agent class, orchestrates classification workflow"
      key_functions:
        - name: "process"
          purpose: "Main entry point for classification requests"
        - name: "_prepare_context"
          purpose: "Prepare input for LLM (formatting, context building)"
        - name: "_track_metrics"
          purpose: "Log classification metrics for monitoring"
          
    - file: "classifier.py"
      purpose: "Core classification logic using LLM"
      key_functions:
        - name: "classify"
          purpose: "Call LLM and parse classification result"
        - name: "_build_prompt"
          purpose: "Construct prompt from context and examples"
          
    - file: "validator.py"
      purpose: "Validate classification output quality"
      key_functions:
        - name: "is_valid"
          purpose: "Check if classification meets quality criteria"
        - name: "validate_category"
          purpose: "Ensure category is in allowed set"
          
  dependencies:
    - "openai or anthropic (LLM provider SDK)"
    - "pydantic (for structured output validation)"
    - "prometheus_client (for metrics tracking)"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# VERIFICATION PATTERN
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

verification_pattern:
  continuous_checks:
    - check: "Classification accuracy"
      frequency: "daily"
      command: "pytest tests/test_accuracy.py::test_classification_accuracy"
      threshold: ">= [constraint from intent]"
      
    - check: "Response latency (p95)"
      frequency: "per_request"
      command: "Monitor APM metrics"
      threshold: "< [constraint from intent]"
      
    - check: "Cost per classification"
      frequency: "daily"
      command: "Review cost tracking dashboard"
      threshold: "< [constraint from intent]"
      
    - check: "Error rate"
      frequency: "hourly"
      command: "Monitor error logs and metrics"
      threshold: "< 5%"
      
  milestone_checks:
    - milestone: "Weekly accuracy review"
      validation: "Review classification accuracy on production data"
      frequency: "weekly"
      
    - milestone: "Monthly performance review"
      validation: "Analyze latency trends, cost trends, accuracy trends"
      frequency: "monthly"
      
    - milestone: "Quarterly model evaluation"
      validation: "Compare current approach against alternatives, consider improvements"
      frequency: "quarterly"
      
  monitoring_setup:
    - metric: "classification_accuracy"
      dashboard: "Grafana/CloudWatch - [Agent Name] Classification Dashboard"
      alert_threshold: "< [constraint - 0.05]"
      
    - metric: "classification_latency_p95"
      dashboard: "APM Dashboard"
      alert_threshold: "> [constraint * 1.5]"
      
    - metric: "classification_cost_per_request"

alidation: "Analyze latency trends, cost trends, accuracy trends"
      frequency: "monthly"
      
    - milestone: "Quarterly model evaluation"
      validation: "Compare current approach against alternatives, consider improvements"
      frequency: "quarterly"
      
  monitoring_setup:
    - metric: "classification_accuracy"
      dashboard: "Grafana/CloudWatch - [Agent Name] Classification Dashboard"
      alert_threshold: "< [constraint - 0.05]"
      
    - metric: "classification_latency_p95"
      dashboard: "APM Dashboard"
      alert_threshold: "> [constraint * 1.5]"
      
    - metric: "classification_cost_per_request"
      dashboard: "Cost Tracking Dashboard"
      alert_threshold: "> [budget * 1.5]"
      
    - metric: "classification_error_rate"
      dashboard: "Error Monitoring Dashboard"
      alert_threshold: "> 5%"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# EXAMPLES
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

examples:
  - name: "Fact Checker Agent (Lore Validation)"
    location: "agent/fact_checker/"
    domain: "Gaming / Content Validation"
    customization: |
      - Added source citation requirements
      - Multi-category validation (accuracy, canonicity, completeness)
      - Integration with gaming knowledge bases
    outcome: "90%+ accuracy on gaming lore fact checking"
    link_to_intent: "agent/fact_checker/fact_checker_intent.yaml"
    
  - name: "Lead Scoring Classifier"
    location: "agent/lead_scorer/"
    domain: "CRM / Sales"
    customization: |
      - Binary classification (qualified / not qualified)
      - Integration with CRM enrichment data
      - Real-time scoring on lead creation
    outcome: "85% precision, 70% recall, 15% sales conversion"
    link_to_intent: "agent/lead_scorer/lead_scorer_intent.yaml"
    
  - name: "Support Ticket Router"
    location: "agent/ticket_router/"
    domain: "Customer Support"
    customization: |
      - Multi-class classification (department routing)
      - Urgency detection as secondary classification
      - Integration with ticketing system
    outcome: "88% routing accuracy, 40% reduction in mis-routed tickets"
    link_to_intent: "agent/ticket_router/ticket_router_intent.yaml"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# BEST PRACTICES
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

best_practices:
  - practice: "Use few-shot examples in prompts"
    why: "Improves classification accuracy by showing LLM what good classifications look like"
    example: "Include 3-5 example classifications in prompt context"
    
  - practice: "Validate output structure before using"
    why: "LLMs occasionally return malformed output, validation prevents downstream errors"
    example: "Use Pydantic models to validate classification response structure"
    
  - practice: "Track metrics on every classification"
    why: "Enables monitoring of drift and performance degradation"
    example: "Log classification, confidence, latency, cost to metrics system"
    
  - practice: "Implement confidence thresholds"
    why: "Low

ation accuracy by showing LLM what good classifications look like"
    example: "Include 3-5 example classifications in prompt context"
    
  - practice: "Validate output structure before using"
    why: "LLMs occasionally return malformed output, validation prevents downstream errors"
    example: "Use Pydantic models to validate classification response structure"
    
  - practice: "Track metrics on every classification"
    why: "Enables monitoring of drift and performance degradation"
    example: "Log classification, confidence, latency, cost to metrics system"
    
  - practice: "Implement confidence thresholds"
    why: "Low confidence classifications should be handled differently (manual review, fallback)"
    example: "If confidence < 0.70, flag for manual review"
    
  - practice: "Cache frequently classified inputs"
    why: "Reduces cost and latency for repeated inputs"
    example: "Cache classification results with TTL based on category stability"
    
  - practice: "Use cheaper models for simple cases"
    why: "Not all classifications need most powerful (expensive) model"
    example: "Use GPT-3.5 for clear cases, GPT-4 only for ambiguous cases"

common_pitfalls:
  - pitfall: "Prompt is too vague or generic"
    symptom: "Classifications are inconsistent or don't match expectations"
    fix: "Add specific examples, constraints, and category definitions to prompt"
    
  - pitfall: "No validation of LLM output"
    symptom: "Agent crashes or returns invalid data to downstream systems"
    fix: "Implement strict output validation using schemas (Pydantic, JSON Schema)"
    
  - pitfall: "Not monitoring accuracy over time"
    symptom: "Classification quality degrades silently, stakeholders complain"
    fix: "Set up automated accuracy tracking with alerts on degradation"
    
  - pitfall: "Treating all classifications equally"
    symptom: "High-value/high-risk classifications get same treatment as low-value ones"
    fix: "Implement confidence thresholds and different handling paths"
    
  - pitfall: "No cost tracking"
    symptom: "Monthly LLM bills surprise you"
    fix: "Track cost per classification, set budgets, optimize prompts"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# COST & PERFORMANCE
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

typical_costs:
  development_time: "2-5 days (with recipe: 1-2 days)"
  infrastructure_cost: "$50-500/month (depends on volume and model)"
  maintenance_effort: "2-4 hours/month (monitoring, prompt updates)"

typical_performance:
  latency: "500ms-3s (depends on LLM model and prompt complexity)"
  throughput: "10-50 classifications/second (with concurrent requests)"
  accuracy: "80-95% (depends on problem complexity and prompt quality)"
